one_liner: CELH at $30.52 in Wyckoff Phase A-B with RSI 53.6, 39% DCF upside, and volume drying — Needham maintained a Buy with a $55 target, offering a clean base-building setup near EMA50 support.

price_action.vol_rank: Below average — low-volume consolidation within base
price_action.intraday_story: CELH opened slightly above its low, ranged tightly between $29.88 and $30.85, and closed at $30.52 in the upper half of the range. Low-volume, tight-range candle — textbook base-building behavior.

news_catalyst.headline: Needham Maintains Buy on CELH, Lowers Price Target to $55
news_catalyst.summary: Needham maintained its Buy rating on Celsius but lowered the price target from a higher level to $55 — still implying 80%+ upside from $30.52. The PT reduction reflects near-term headwinds from distribution rationalization, but long-term conviction in the energy drink brand's market share gains remains intact.

technical.summary: CELH is in early Wyckoff accumulation (Phase A-B) with the engine detecting a bullish bias. EMA50 at $29.38 and EMA200 at $28.67 are both below current price — the stock is above both EMAs, a positive structure. Wyckoff support at $27.74 provides a defined risk level. Volume drying on the consolidation is constructive. DCF suggests 39% upside at $42.46.
technical.wyckoff_narrative: Phase A-B accumulation with bullish bias. Watching for a high-volume break above $33.05 (upper BB) as Sign of Strength. Wyckoff support $27.74 is the invalidation level.

valuation.summary: DCF $42 vs $30 — 39% upside. Needham's $55 target is more bullish. Grade B fundamentals support accumulation in the base.

key_strengths: Energy drink brand with expanding retail distribution
Strong youth demographic loyalty and brand momentum
key_concerns: Competition from Monster and Red Bull intensifying
fundamentals.summary: Grade B (0.796) — solid fundamentals for a consumer discretionary growth name. Needham's Buy with $55 target validates the accumulate stance.

risks: Close below EMA50 ($29.38) would break the accumulation pattern
Distribution rationalization — any further customer loss would hit revenue growth
